rationale: P0 coverage is 100% (required: 100%), P1 coverage is 100% (target: 90%), overall coverage is 100% (minimum: 80%). All 6 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC6) are fully covered by 18 new passing unit tests (10 SkillLoader ATDD + 5 SkillTypes ATDD + 3 code-review regression fixes) plus 4 legacy parseAllowedTools regression tests that continue to pass. The ToolDeclaration / ToolDeclarationStatus / ToolDeclarationDiagnostics data model and parseToolDeclarations parser passed 3-layer adversarial code review with 3 in-scope correctness fixes applied (F1 empty-parens phantom pattern, F6 unclosed-paren silent demotion, F9 MCP-with-trailing-pattern normalized-name corruption), each with a dedicated regression test. Production code verified: SkillLoader.loadSkillFromDirectory dual-populates legacy toolRestrictions + new toolDeclarations/toolDeclarationDiagnostics (AC4 backward compatibility). No ToolRestriction.task case added (Dev Notes gap compliance). No critical, high, medium, or low coverage gaps identified. Full suite at 5738/5738 passing (baseline 5720 -> +18). Story status `done` (set by code-review step) confirmed correct.

AC1.description: MCP namespaced tool declarations preserved (mcp__github__list_prs not dropped)

AC2.description: Unknown tool names do not collapse to unrestricted (returns non-nil)

AC3.description: Permission pattern text preserved and annotated (Bash(git diff:*))

AC4.description: Backward compatibility - existing Skill.toolRestrictions field and consumers have no regression

AC5.description: Common SDK/Claude tool names recognized (.recognizedSDK); Task gap handled

AC6.description: Build + full regression (zero warnings, all tests pass)
